model_narrative: We plan investment and production for the world copper sector for a single base year. Across many regions we decide how intensely to run each mining, smelting, refining and semi-manufacturing process, how much to ship ore, blister, refined copper and finished semi-manufactures between locations and to markets, how much scrap to supply, and which productive units to expand and by how much, with expansion decisions being yes-or-no choices that then permit a continuous amount of added capacity. A set of accounting quantities gather the capital charges, operating costs, transport costs and tariffs into a single total. The objective is to minimize that total annual cost including tariffs.

sets.doc: mine, smelter and refinery locations

sets.name: j

sets.doc: commodities present at wire, tube and sheet plants

sets.name: cfs

params.name: sigma
params.doc: capital recovery factor applied to capital charges, a scalar

params.name: rph
params.doc: reserve-horizon period length in years, a scalar equal to 20

params.name: a
params.doc: input-output coefficients of each commodity in each process; positive for an output of the process and negative for an input it consumes; zero where the commodity is not involved

params.name: b
params.doc: capacity utilization matrix giving how much of a productive unit each process consumes per unit of activity; zero where the unit is not used by the process

params.name: mapic
params.doc: equals 1 if the commodity is present at the mine/smelter/refinery location and 0 otherwise; used to decide which material balances exist there

params.name: mapip
params.doc: equals 1 if the process is available at the mine/smelter/refinery location and 0 otherwise; processes whose mask is 0 are not run there

params.name: mapjc
params.doc: equals 1 if the commodity is present at the wire/tube/sheet plant location and 0 otherwise

params.name: mapjp
params.doc: equals 1 if the semi-manufacturing process is available at the plant location and 0 otherwise

params.name: demand
params.doc: required quantity of each final product at each market location, in thousand tons

params.name: capm
params.doc: existing capacity of each productive unit at each mine/smelter/refinery location

params.name: caps
params.doc: existing capacity of each productive unit at each semi-manufacturing location

params.name: hhatm
params.doc: maximum permissible plant-size expansion of a productive unit at a mine/smelter/refinery location

params.name: hhats
params.doc: maximum permissible plant-size expansion of a productive unit at a semi-manufacturing location

params.name: hbarm
params.doc: economies-of-scale reference size for an expansion at a mine/smelter/refinery location

params.name: hbars
params.doc: economies-of-scale reference size for an expansion at a semi-manufacturing location

params.name: omegam
params.doc: fixed scale-related capital cost coefficient for mines/smelters/refineries

params.name: num
params.doc: proportional (per-unit-of-expansion) capital cost coefficient for mines/smelters/refineries

params.name: omegas
params.doc: fixed scale-related capital cost coefficient for semi-manufacturing plants

params.name: nus
params.doc: proportional (per-unit-of-expansion) capital cost coefficient for semi-manufacturing plants

params.name: opm
params.doc: operating cost per unit of process activity at mines/smelters/refineries

params.name: ops
params.doc: operating cost per unit of process activity at semi-manufacturing plants

params.name: mur
params.doc: transport cost per unit of ore or blister shipped between two mine/smelter/refinery locations

params.name: mufs

params.name: mui

vars.name: sm

vars.name: hs
vars.doc: amount of capacity expansion of a productive unit at a semi-manufacturing location

vars.name: ss

vars.name: ys

vars.name: phiks
vars.doc: accounting variable holding total capital charges for semi-manufacturing plants

vars.doc: accounting variable holding total operating cost at mines/smelters/refineries

vars.name: phios
vars.doc: accounting variable holding total operating cost at semi-manufacturing plants

constraints.name: mapic[i,
constraints.intent: At each mine, smelter and refinery location, and for each material that is present there, the material has to balance. Add up the net amount of that material produced by the processes run at the location, counting each process by how much of the material it makes or uses. For ore and blister also add what is shipped in from other locations, and for scrap materials add the scrap supplied locally. This available amount must be at least what is needed, which for refined copper is everything shipped out from the location to plants and to markets, and for ore and blister is everything shipped from this location out to other locations. Locations where a material is not present have no such requirement.

constraints.name: mapjc[j,
constraints.intent: At each wire, tube and sheet plant, and for each material present there, the material has to balance. Add up the net amount of that material produced by the semi-manufacturing processes run at the plant, and for refined copper also add what is shipped in from smelters and refineries, and for scrap materials add the scrap supplied at the plant. This available amount must be at least the amount of finished semi-manufactures shipped out from the plant to markets. Plants where a material is not present have no such requirement.

constraints.name: cfr:
constraints.intent: Every market must have its demand met for each final product. For each location and each final product, the total amount delivered there must be at least the demand. Refined copper is delivered as refined copper shipments arriving at the location, while each semi-manufactured product is delivered as shipments of that product arriving from the plants.

constraints.name: b[mm,
constraints.intent: No mine, smelter or refinery may run its processes beyond the capacity of each of its productive units. For each location and each of its productive units, the total use of that unit across all the processes run there must not exceed the existing capacity of the unit plus any capacity added by expansion.

constraints.name: b[ms,
constraints.intent: No semi-manufacturing plant may run its processes beyond the capacity of each of its productive units. For each plant and each of its productive units, the total use of that unit across all the processes run there must not exceed the existing capacity of the unit plus any capacity added by expansion.

constraints.name: hm[mm,
constraints.intent: Expanding a productive unit at a mine, smelter or refinery is only allowed if the location decides to expand that unit, and even then the expansion cannot exceed an allowed maximum size. For each location and each of its productive units, the amount of expansion is held to zero unless the expansion decision is taken, in which case it may go up to the maximum permissible plant size.

constraints.name: hs[ms,
constraints.intent: Expanding a productive unit at a semi-manufacturing plant is only allowed if the plant decides to expand that unit, and even then the expansion cannot exceed an allowed maximum size. For each plant and each of its productive units, the amount of expansion is held to zero unless the expansion decision is taken, in which case it may go up to the maximum permissible plant size.

constraints.name: hm[mm,
constraints.intent: When a productive unit at a mine, smelter or refinery is expanded, the expansion must reach the economies-of-scale reference size, with any shortfall taken up by a slack quantity. For each location and each of its productive units, the expansion amount together with its unused-scale slack must be at least the reference size whenever the expansion decision is taken, and the requirement is inactive when it is not.

constraints.name: hs[ms,
constraints.intent: When a productive unit at a semi-manufacturing plant is expanded, the expansion must reach the economies-of-scale reference size, with any shortfall taken up by a slack quantity. For each plant and each of its productive units, the expansion amount together with its unused-scale slack must be at least the reference size whenever the expansion decision is taken, and the requirement is inactive when it is not.

constraints.name: rph
constraints.intent: Mining cannot draw down more ore than the reserves allow over the planning horizon. For each ore mining process at each location, the process activity run over the whole reserve horizon must stay within the ore reserves available to that process at that location.

constraints.name: ssa[j]
constraints.intent: At each semi-manufacturing location, the total scrap supplied there equals the sum of the scrap supplied of every individual scrap type at that location.

constraints.name: phikm
constraints.intent: The total capital charges for mines, smelters and refineries are defined by their cost drivers. Summed over every location and each of its productive units, take the fixed scale-related cost applied to the unused-scale slack plus the proportional cost applied to the amount of expansion, scale the whole sum by the capital recovery factor, and set the mine and smelter and refinery capital-charge accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: phiks
constraints.intent: The total capital charges for semi-manufacturing plants are defined by their cost drivers. Summed over every plant and each of its productive units, take the fixed scale-related cost applied to the unused-scale slack plus the proportional cost applied to the amount of expansion, scale the whole sum by the capital recovery factor, and set the semi-manufacturing capital-charge accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: phiom
constraints.intent: The total operating cost at mines, smelters and refineries is defined by process activity. Summed over every location and each process actually run there, multiply the operating cost of the process by its activity level, then divide the total by one thousand, and set the mine and smelter and refinery operating-cost accounting quantity equal to that amount.

constraints.name: phios
constraints.intent: The total operating cost at semi-manufacturing plants is defined by process activity. Summed over every plant and each semi-manufacturing process, multiply the operating cost of the process by its activity level, and set the semi-manufacturing operating-cost accounting quantity equal to that total.

constraints.name: mur[i,
constraints.intent: The total transport cost adds up the cost of moving everything that is shipped. Take the cost of shipping ore and blister between mine, smelter and refinery locations, plus the cost of shipping finished semi-manufactures between plant and market locations, plus the cost of shipping refined copper from smelters and refineries both to plants and to markets, each priced at its own per-unit shipping rate. Divide the combined total by one thousand and set the transport-cost accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: tariffr[i,
constraints.intent: The total tariff cost adds up the tariffs charged on what crosses borders. Take the tariff on refined copper, charged on both the refined copper sent to markets and the refined copper sent to plants, plus the tariff on semi-manufactured goods moved between locations, each priced at its own per-unit tariff rate. Divide the combined total by one thousand and set the tariff-cost accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: phiutf
constraints.intent: The total annual undiscounted cost with tariffs is the sum of all the cost components: the capital charges for mines, smelters and refineries, the capital charges for semi-manufacturing plants, the operating costs at both kinds of location, the transport cost and the tariff cost. Set the undiscounted-total accounting quantity equal to that sum.

constraints.name: phi2
constraints.intent: The total cost that is minimized equals the total annual undiscounted cost with tariffs. Set the final cost accounting quantity equal to the undiscounted-total accounting quantity.

query: There's a proposal on the table to throttle back how aggressively the whole network mines its high-grade ore — the idea is to keep total high-grade mining activity, summed across every location, under roughly 44 times the combined high-grade reserve base. What does the optimizer return for total network cost if we cap the network-wide high-grade draw that way?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_high_grade_total = Constraint(expr=sum(model.zm['high-grade', i] for i in model.i) <= 44 * sum(model.reserves[i, 'high-grade'] for i in model.i))

query: We're looking at a service-coverage rule where no market is left thinly supplied with refined copper — every consumer market should pull in at least 30 units of refined copper from somewhere in the network. If we hold a 30-unit refined-copper floor at each market, what total cost does the optimizer come back with?

params.name: sigma
params.doc: capital recovery factor applied to capital charges, a scalar

params.name: rph
params.doc: reserve-horizon period length in years, a scalar equal to 20

params.name: a
params.doc: input-output coefficients of each commodity in each process; positive for an output of the process and negative for an input it consumes; zero where the commodity is not involved

params.name: b
params.doc: capacity utilization matrix giving how much of a productive unit each process consumes per unit of activity; zero where the unit is not used by the process

params.name: mapic
params.doc: equals 1 if the commodity is present at the mine/smelter/refinery location and 0 otherwise; used to decide which material balances exist there

params.name: mapip
params.doc: equals 1 if the process is available at the mine/smelter/refinery location and 0 otherwise; processes whose mask is 0 are not run there

params.name: mapjc
params.doc: equals 1 if the commodity is present at the wire/tube/sheet plant location and 0 otherwise

params.name: mapjp
params.doc: equals 1 if the semi-manufacturing process is available at the plant location and 0 otherwise

params.name: demand
params.doc: required quantity of each final product at each market location, in thousand tons

params.name: capm
params.doc: existing capacity of each productive unit at each mine/smelter/refinery location

params.name: caps
params.doc: existing capacity of each productive unit at each semi-manufacturing location

params.name: hhatm
params.doc: maximum permissible plant-size expansion of a productive unit at a mine/smelter/refinery location

params.name: hhats
params.doc: maximum permissible plant-size expansion of a productive unit at a semi-manufacturing location

params.name: hbarm
params.doc: economies-of-scale reference size for an expansion at a mine/smelter/refinery location

params.name: hbars
params.doc: economies-of-scale reference size for an expansion at a semi-manufacturing location

params.name: omegam
params.doc: fixed scale-related capital cost coefficient for mines/smelters/refineries

params.name: num
params.doc: proportional (per-unit-of-expansion) capital cost coefficient for mines/smelters/refineries

params.name: omegas
params.doc: fixed scale-related capital cost coefficient for semi-manufacturing plants

params.name: nus
params.doc: proportional (per-unit-of-expansion) capital cost coefficient for semi-manufacturing plants

params.name: opm
params.doc: operating cost per unit of process activity at mines/smelters/refineries

params.name: ops
params.doc: operating cost per unit of process activity at semi-manufacturing plants

params.name: mur
params.doc: transport cost per unit of ore or blister shipped between two mine/smelter/refinery locations

params.name: mufs
params.doc: transport cost per unit of semi-manufactures shipped between two plant/market locations

params.name: mui
params.doc: transport cost per unit of refined copper shipped from a smelter/refinery to a plant or market

params.name: tariffr
params.doc: tariff per unit of refined copper moved from origin to destination

params.name: tariffs
params.doc: tariff per unit of semi-manufactured goods moved from origin to destination

vars.name: zm

vars.name: zs

vars.name: xi

vars.name: xfr

vars.name: xfs

vars.name: ssr
vars.doc: scrap of each type supplied at smelters and refineries

vars.name: ssm

vars.name: ssa

vars.name: hm
vars.doc: amount of capacity expansion of a productive unit at a mine/smelter/refinery location

vars.name: sm

vars.name: hs
vars.doc: amount of capacity expansion of a productive unit at a semi-manufacturing location

vars.name: ss
vars.doc: unused economies-of-scale slack for an expansion at a semi-manufacturing location

vars.name: ys
vars.doc: equals 1 if a productive unit is expanded at a semi-manufacturing location and 0 otherwise

vars.name: phikm
vars.doc: accounting variable holding total capital charges for mines/smelters/refineries

vars.name: phiks
vars.doc: accounting variable holding total capital charges for semi-manufacturing plants

vars.name: phiom
vars.doc: accounting variable holding total operating cost at mines/smelters/refineries

vars.name: phios
vars.doc: accounting variable holding total operating cost at semi-manufacturing plants

vars.name: phit
vars.doc: accounting variable holding total transport cost

vars.name: phitf

constraints.name: mapic[i,
constraints.intent: At each mine, smelter and refinery location, and for each material that is present there, the material has to balance. Add up the net amount of that material produced by the processes run at the location, counting each process by how much of the material it makes or uses. For ore and blister also add what is shipped in from other locations, and for scrap materials add the scrap supplied locally. This available amount must be at least what is needed, which for refined copper is everything shipped out from the location to plants and to markets, and for ore and blister is everything shipped from this location out to other locations. Locations where a material is not present have no such requirement.

constraints.name: mapjc[j,
constraints.intent: At each wire, tube and sheet plant, and for each material present there, the material has to balance. Add up the net amount of that material produced by the semi-manufacturing processes run at the plant, and for refined copper also add what is shipped in from smelters and refineries, and for scrap materials add the scrap supplied at the plant. This available amount must be at least the amount of finished semi-manufactures shipped out from the plant to markets. Plants where a material is not present have no such requirement.

constraints.name: cfr:
constraints.intent: Every market must have its demand met for each final product. For each location and each final product, the total amount delivered there must be at least the demand. Refined copper is delivered as refined copper shipments arriving at the location, while each semi-manufactured product is delivered as shipments of that product arriving from the plants.

constraints.name: b[mm,
constraints.intent: No mine, smelter or refinery may run its processes beyond the capacity of each of its productive units. For each location and each of its productive units, the total use of that unit across all the processes run there must not exceed the existing capacity of the unit plus any capacity added by expansion.

constraints.name: b[ms,
constraints.intent: No semi-manufacturing plant may run its processes beyond the capacity of each of its productive units. For each plant and each of its productive units, the total use of that unit across all the processes run there must not exceed the existing capacity of the unit plus any capacity added by expansion.

constraints.name: hm[mm,
constraints.intent: Expanding a productive unit at a mine, smelter or refinery is only allowed if the location decides to expand that unit, and even then the expansion cannot exceed an allowed maximum size. For each location and each of its productive units, the amount of expansion is held to zero unless the expansion decision is taken, in which case it may go up to the maximum permissible plant size.

constraints.name: hs[ms,
constraints.intent: Expanding a productive unit at a semi-manufacturing plant is only allowed if the plant decides to expand that unit, and even then the expansion cannot exceed an allowed maximum size. For each plant and each of its productive units, the amount of expansion is held to zero unless the expansion decision is taken, in which case it may go up to the maximum permissible plant size.

constraints.name: hm[mm,
constraints.intent: When a productive unit at a mine, smelter or refinery is expanded, the expansion must reach the economies-of-scale reference size, with any shortfall taken up by a slack quantity. For each location and each of its productive units, the expansion amount together with its unused-scale slack must be at least the reference size whenever the expansion decision is taken, and the requirement is inactive when it is not.

constraints.name: hs[ms,
constraints.intent: When a productive unit at a semi-manufacturing plant is expanded, the expansion must reach the economies-of-scale reference size, with any shortfall taken up by a slack quantity. For each plant and each of its productive units, the expansion amount together with its unused-scale slack must be at least the reference size whenever the expansion decision is taken, and the requirement is inactive when it is not.

constraints.name: rph
constraints.intent: Mining cannot draw down more ore than the reserves allow over the planning horizon. For each ore mining process at each location, the process activity run over the whole reserve horizon must stay within the ore reserves available to that process at that location.

constraints.name: ssa[j]
constraints.intent: At each semi-manufacturing location, the total scrap supplied there equals the sum of the scrap supplied of every individual scrap type at that location.

constraints.name: phikm
constraints.intent: The total capital charges for mines, smelters and refineries are defined by their cost drivers. Summed over every location and each of its productive units, take the fixed scale-related cost applied to the unused-scale slack plus the proportional cost applied to the amount of expansion, scale the whole sum by the capital recovery factor, and set the mine and smelter and refinery capital-charge accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: phiks
constraints.intent: The total capital charges for semi-manufacturing plants are defined by their cost drivers. Summed over every plant and each of its productive units, take the fixed scale-related cost applied to the unused-scale slack plus the proportional cost applied to the amount of expansion, scale the whole sum by the capital recovery factor, and set the semi-manufacturing capital-charge accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: phiom
constraints.intent: The total operating cost at mines, smelters and refineries is defined by process activity. Summed over every location and each process actually run there, multiply the operating cost of the process by its activity level, then divide the total by one thousand, and set the mine and smelter and refinery operating-cost accounting quantity equal to that amount.

constraints.name: phios
constraints.intent: The total operating cost at semi-manufacturing plants is defined by process activity. Summed over every plant and each semi-manufacturing process, multiply the operating cost of the process by its activity level, and set the semi-manufacturing operating-cost accounting quantity equal to that total.

constraints.name: mur[i,
constraints.intent: The total transport cost adds up the cost of moving everything that is shipped. Take the cost of shipping ore and blister between mine, smelter and refinery locations, plus the cost of shipping finished semi-manufactures between plant and market locations, plus the cost of shipping refined copper from smelters and refineries both to plants and to markets, each priced at its own per-unit shipping rate. Divide the combined total by one thousand and set the transport-cost accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: tariffr[i,
constraints.intent: The total tariff cost adds up the tariffs charged on what crosses borders. Take the tariff on refined copper, charged on both the refined copper sent to markets and the refined copper sent to plants, plus the tariff on semi-manufactured goods moved between locations, each priced at its own per-unit tariff rate. Divide the combined total by one thousand and set the tariff-cost accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: phiutf
constraints.intent: The total annual undiscounted cost with tariffs is the sum of all the cost components: the capital charges for mines, smelters and refineries, the capital charges for semi-manufacturing plants, the operating costs at both kinds of location, the transport cost and the tariff cost. Set the undiscounted-total accounting quantity equal to that sum.

constraints.name: phi2
constraints.intent: The total cost that is minimized equals the total annual undiscounted cost with tariffs. Set the final cost accounting quantity equal to the undiscounted-total accounting quantity.

query: The board is pitching an ore-diversification guardrail: across the whole network, high-grade ore mining shouldn't make up more than 55% of total ore mining when you add high-grade and medium-grade activity together. If we hold high-grade's share of total mined ore under that 55% line, what does the optimizer return for total network cost?
expected_pyomo: model.high_grade_share = Constraint(expr=sum(model.zm['high-grade', i] for i in model.i) <= 0.55 * sum(model.zm['high-grade', i] + model.zm['med-grade', i] for i in model.i))

model_narrative: We plan investment and production for the world copper sector for a single base year. Across many regions we decide how intensely to run each mining, smelting, refining and semi-manufacturing process, how much to ship ore, blister, refined copper and finished semi-manufactures between locations and to markets, how much scrap to supply, and which productive units to expand and by how much, with expansion decisions being yes-or-no choices that then permit a continuous amount of added capacity. A set of accounting quantities gather the capital charges, operating costs, transport costs and tariffs into a single total. The objective is to minimize that total annual cost including tariffs.

query: A few of our mines are getting hammered far harder than their reserve base really justifies — the plan runs high-grade extraction at some sites right up against the geological ceiling. Shouldn't every location's high-grade mining be held to a more conservative level relative to its own high-grade reserves, no more than forty units of activity per unit of reserve, so we're not over-mining the richest pits? What's the model seeing that makes pushing those mines so hard worth it?

constraints.name: mapic[i,
constraints.intent: At each mine, smelter and refinery location, and for each material that is present there, the material has to balance. Add up the net amount of that material produced by the processes run at the location, counting each process by how much of the material it makes or uses. For ore and blister also add what is shipped in from other locations, and for scrap materials add the scrap supplied locally. This available amount must be at least what is needed, which for refined copper is everything shipped out from the location to plants and to markets, and for ore and blister is everything shipped from this location out to other locations. Locations where a material is not present have no such requirement.

constraints.name: mapjc[j,
constraints.intent: At each wire, tube and sheet plant, and for each material present there, the material has to balance. Add up the net amount of that material produced by the semi-manufacturing processes run at the plant, and for refined copper also add what is shipped in from smelters and refineries, and for scrap materials add the scrap supplied at the plant. This available amount must be at least the amount of finished semi-manufactures shipped out from the plant to markets. Plants where a material is not present have no such requirement.

constraints.name: cfr:
constraints.intent: Every market must have its demand met for each final product. For each location and each final product, the total amount delivered there must be at least the demand. Refined copper is delivered as refined copper shipments arriving at the location, while each semi-manufactured product is delivered as shipments of that product arriving from the plants.

constraints.name: b[mm,
constraints.intent: No mine, smelter or refinery may run its processes beyond the capacity of each of its productive units. For each location and each of its productive units, the total use of that unit across all the processes run there must not exceed the existing capacity of the unit plus any capacity added by expansion.

constraints.name: b[ms,
constraints.intent: No semi-manufacturing plant may run its processes beyond the capacity of each of its productive units. For each plant and each of its productive units, the total use of that unit across all the processes run there must not exceed the existing capacity of the unit plus any capacity added by expansion.

constraints.name: hm[mm,
constraints.intent: Expanding a productive unit at a mine, smelter or refinery is only allowed if the location decides to expand that unit, and even then the expansion cannot exceed an allowed maximum size. For each location and each of its productive units, the amount of expansion is held to zero unless the expansion decision is taken, in which case it may go up to the maximum permissible plant size.

constraints.name: hs[ms,
constraints.intent: Expanding a productive unit at a semi-manufacturing plant is only allowed if the plant decides to expand that unit, and even then the expansion cannot exceed an allowed maximum size. For each plant and each of its productive units, the amount of expansion is held to zero unless the expansion decision is taken, in which case it may go up to the maximum permissible plant size.

constraints.name: hm[mm,
constraints.intent: When a productive unit at a mine, smelter or refinery is expanded, the expansion must reach the economies-of-scale reference size, with any shortfall taken up by a slack quantity. For each location and each of its productive units, the expansion amount together with its unused-scale slack must be at least the reference size whenever the expansion decision is taken, and the requirement is inactive when it is not.

constraints.name: hs[ms,
constraints.intent: When a productive unit at a semi-manufacturing plant is expanded, the expansion must reach the economies-of-scale reference size, with any shortfall taken up by a slack quantity. For each plant and each of its productive units, the expansion amount together with its unused-scale slack must be at least the reference size whenever the expansion decision is taken, and the requirement is inactive when it is not.

constraints.name: rph
constraints.intent: Mining cannot draw down more ore than the reserves allow over the planning horizon. For each ore mining process at each location, the process activity run over the whole reserve horizon must stay within the ore reserves available to that process at that location.

constraints.name: ssa[j]
constraints.intent: At each semi-manufacturing location, the total scrap supplied there equals the sum of the scrap supplied of every individual scrap type at that location.

constraints.name: phikm
constraints.intent: The total capital charges for mines, smelters and refineries are defined by their cost drivers. Summed over every location and each of its productive units, take the fixed scale-related cost applied to the unused-scale slack plus the proportional cost applied to the amount of expansion, scale the whole sum by the capital recovery factor, and set the mine and smelter and refinery capital-charge accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: phiks
constraints.intent: The total capital charges for semi-manufacturing plants are defined by their cost drivers. Summed over every plant and each of its productive units, take the fixed scale-related cost applied to the unused-scale slack plus the proportional cost applied to the amount of expansion, scale the whole sum by the capital recovery factor, and set the semi-manufacturing capital-charge accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: phiom
constraints.intent: The total operating cost at mines, smelters and refineries is defined by process activity. Summed over every location and each process actually run there, multiply the operating cost of the process by its activity level, then divide the total by one thousand, and set the mine and smelter and refinery operating-cost accounting quantity equal to that amount.

constraints.name: phios
constraints.intent: The total operating cost at semi-manufacturing plants is defined by process activity. Summed over every plant and each semi-manufacturing process, multiply the operating cost of the process by its activity level, and set the semi-manufacturing operating-cost accounting quantity equal to that total.

constraints.name: mur[i,
constraints.intent: The total transport cost adds up the cost of moving everything that is shipped. Take the cost of shipping ore and blister between mine, smelter and refinery locations, plus the cost of shipping finished semi-manufactures between plant and market locations, plus the cost of shipping refined copper from smelters and refineries both to plants and to markets, each priced at its own per-unit shipping rate. Divide the combined total by one thousand and set the transport-cost accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: tariffr[i,
constraints.intent: The total tariff cost adds up the tariffs charged on what crosses borders. Take the tariff on refined copper, charged on both the refined copper sent to markets and the refined copper sent to plants, plus the tariff on semi-manufactured goods moved between locations, each priced at its own per-unit tariff rate. Divide the combined total by one thousand and set the tariff-cost accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: phiutf
constraints.intent: The total annual undiscounted cost with tariffs is the sum of all the cost components: the capital charges for mines, smelters and refineries, the capital charges for semi-manufacturing plants, the operating costs at both kinds of location, the transport cost and the tariff cost. Set the undiscounted-total accounting quantity equal to that sum.

constraints.name: phi2
constraints.intent: The total cost that is minimized equals the total annual undiscounted cost with tariffs. Set the final cost accounting quantity equal to the undiscounted-total accounting quantity.

query: The Canada wire-and-sheet plants barely move any product — total wire semi-manufactures landing in the Canada market, from all the plants feeding it, come in pretty thin. Wouldn't it be obvious to commit at least 300 units of wire semi-manufactures into the Canada market and put that local capacity to work? What's keeping the optimizer from routing more wire there?
expected_pyomo: model.wire_canada_floor = Constraint(expr=sum(model.xfs['wire', jp, 'canada'] for jp in model.j) >= 300)

model_narrative: We plan investment and production for the world copper sector for a single base year. Across many regions we decide how intensely to run each mining, smelting, refining and semi-manufacturing process, how much to ship ore, blister, refined copper and finished semi-manufactures between locations and to markets, how much scrap to supply, and which productive units to expand and by how much, with expansion decisions being yes-or-no choices that then permit a continuous amount of added capacity. A set of accounting quantities gather the capital charges, operating costs, transport costs and tariffs into a single total. The objective is to minimize that total annual cost including tariffs.

query: We keep leaning the whole mining base on the rich high-grade pits and letting the medium-grade reserves sit underused. Couldn't we keep total medium-grade mining at no less than seventy percent of total high-grade mining across the network, so we're spreading the draw more evenly between the two ore classes? What's pulling the optimizer so hard toward the high-grade ore?
expected_pyomo: model.med_grade_ratio = Constraint(expr=sum(model.zm['med-grade', i] for i in model.i) >= 0.7 * sum(model.zm['high-grade', i] for i in model.i))

params.name: a

params.name: b

params.name: mapic
params.doc: equals 1 if the commodity is present at the mine/smelter/refinery location and 0 otherwise; used to decide which material balances exist there

constraints.name: mapic[i,
constraints.intent: At each mine, smelter and refinery location, and for each material that is present there, the material has to balance. Add up the net amount of that material produced by the processes run at the location, counting each process by how much of the material it makes or uses. For ore and blister also add what is shipped in from other locations, and for scrap materials add the scrap supplied locally. This available amount must be at least what is needed, which for refined copper is everything shipped out from the location to plants and to markets, and for ore and blister is everything shipped from this location out to other locations. Locations where a material is not present have no such requirement.

constraints.name: mapjc[j,
constraints.intent: At each wire, tube and sheet plant, and for each material present there, the material has to balance. Add up the net amount of that material produced by the semi-manufacturing processes run at the plant, and for refined copper also add what is shipped in from smelters and refineries, and for scrap materials add the scrap supplied at the plant. This available amount must be at least the amount of finished semi-manufactures shipped out from the plant to markets. Plants where a material is not present have no such requirement.

constraints.name: cfr:
constraints.intent: Every market must have its demand met for each final product. For each location and each final product, the total amount delivered there must be at least the demand. Refined copper is delivered as refined copper shipments arriving at the location, while each semi-manufactured product is delivered as shipments of that product arriving from the plants.

constraints.name: b[mm,
constraints.intent: No mine, smelter or refinery may run its processes beyond the capacity of each of its productive units. For each location and each of its productive units, the total use of that unit across all the processes run there must not exceed the existing capacity of the unit plus any capacity added by expansion.

constraints.name: b[ms,
constraints.intent: No semi-manufacturing plant may run its processes beyond the capacity of each of its productive units. For each plant and each of its productive units, the total use of that unit across all the processes run there must not exceed the existing capacity of the unit plus any capacity added by expansion.

constraints.name: hm[mm,
constraints.intent: Expanding a productive unit at a mine, smelter or refinery is only allowed if the location decides to expand that unit, and even then the expansion cannot exceed an allowed maximum size. For each location and each of its productive units, the amount of expansion is held to zero unless the expansion decision is taken, in which case it may go up to the maximum permissible plant size.

constraints.name: hs[ms,
constraints.intent: Expanding a productive unit at a semi-manufacturing plant is only allowed if the plant decides to expand that unit, and even then the expansion cannot exceed an allowed maximum size. For each plant and each of its productive units, the amount of expansion is held to zero unless the expansion decision is taken, in which case it may go up to the maximum permissible plant size.

constraints.name: hm[mm,
constraints.intent: When a productive unit at a mine, smelter or refinery is expanded, the expansion must reach the economies-of-scale reference size, with any shortfall taken up by a slack quantity. For each location and each of its productive units, the expansion amount together with its unused-scale slack must be at least the reference size whenever the expansion decision is taken, and the requirement is inactive when it is not.

constraints.name: hs[ms,
constraints.intent: When a productive unit at a semi-manufacturing plant is expanded, the expansion must reach the economies-of-scale reference size, with any shortfall taken up by a slack quantity. For each plant and each of its productive units, the expansion amount together with its unused-scale slack must be at least the reference size whenever the expansion decision is taken, and the requirement is inactive when it is not.

constraints.name: rph
constraints.intent: Mining cannot draw down more ore than the reserves allow over the planning horizon. For each ore mining process at each location, the process activity run over the whole reserve horizon must stay within the ore reserves available to that process at that location.

constraints.name: ssa[j]
constraints.intent: At each semi-manufacturing location, the total scrap supplied there equals the sum of the scrap supplied of every individual scrap type at that location.

constraints.name: phikm
constraints.intent: The total capital charges for mines, smelters and refineries are defined by their cost drivers. Summed over every location and each of its productive units, take the fixed scale-related cost applied to the unused-scale slack plus the proportional cost applied to the amount of expansion, scale the whole sum by the capital recovery factor, and set the mine and smelter and refinery capital-charge accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: phiks
constraints.intent: The total capital charges for semi-manufacturing plants are defined by their cost drivers. Summed over every plant and each of its productive units, take the fixed scale-related cost applied to the unused-scale slack plus the proportional cost applied to the amount of expansion, scale the whole sum by the capital recovery factor, and set the semi-manufacturing capital-charge accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: phiom
constraints.intent: The total operating cost at mines, smelters and refineries is defined by process activity. Summed over every location and each process actually run there, multiply the operating cost of the process by its activity level, then divide the total by one thousand, and set the mine and smelter and refinery operating-cost accounting quantity equal to that amount.

constraints.name: phios
constraints.intent: The total operating cost at semi-manufacturing plants is defined by process activity. Summed over every plant and each semi-manufacturing process, multiply the operating cost of the process by its activity level, and set the semi-manufacturing operating-cost accounting quantity equal to that total.

constraints.name: mur[i,
constraints.intent: The total transport cost adds up the cost of moving everything that is shipped. Take the cost of shipping ore and blister between mine, smelter and refinery locations, plus the cost of shipping finished semi-manufactures between plant and market locations, plus the cost of shipping refined copper from smelters and refineries both to plants and to markets, each priced at its own per-unit shipping rate. Divide the combined total by one thousand and set the transport-cost accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: tariffr[i,
constraints.intent: The total tariff cost adds up the tariffs charged on what crosses borders. Take the tariff on refined copper, charged on both the refined copper sent to markets and the refined copper sent to plants, plus the tariff on semi-manufactured goods moved between locations, each priced at its own per-unit tariff rate. Divide the combined total by one thousand and set the tariff-cost accounting quantity equal to it.

constraints.name: phiutf
constraints.intent: The total annual undiscounted cost with tariffs is the sum of all the cost components: the capital charges for mines, smelters and refineries, the capital charges for semi-manufacturing plants, the operating costs at both kinds of location, the transport cost and the tariff cost. Set the undiscounted-total accounting quantity equal to that sum.

constraints.name: phi2
constraints.intent: The total cost that is minimized equals the total annual undiscounted cost with tariffs. Set the final cost accounting quantity equal to the undiscounted-total accounting quantity.

query: Two things about this plan bother our planning group at once: smaller markets get left thin on refined copper, and the mining base leans too heavily on the high-grade pits. Couldn't we run it as one package — guarantee every market at least 25 units of refined copper, and at the same time keep high-grade ore under 60% of total high-grade-plus-medium-grade mining? What's the model trading off that keeps it from accepting both of those at once?
expected_pyomo:
def _market_refined_floor(model, j):
    return sum(model.xfr[i, j] for i in model.i) >= 25
model.market_refined_floor = Constraint(model.j, rule=_market_refined_floor)
model.high_grade_share = Constraint(expr=sum(model.zm['high-grade', i] for i in model.i) <= 0.6 * sum(model.zm['high-grade', i] + model.zm['med-grade', i] for i in model.i))
